# ATS Optimization Rules

## Keyword Matching

- Extract every required skill, tool, and qualification from the JD — these MUST appear verbatim if the candidate has them
- Use exact JD spelling (e.g., "Node.js" not "NodeJS" if JD says "Node.js")
- Include preferred/nice-to-have skills if candidate has them
- Align resume title to JD title in summary if they differ

**Keyword placement priority:** Skills section > most recent role bullets > summary > older bullets

## ATS Formatting

- Standard section headers: `Experience`, `Education`, `Skills`, `Projects`, `Certifications`
- Plain bullet points only — no tables, columns, text boxes, graphics, or sidebars
- Spell out abbreviations on first use
- Date format: `Jan 2022 – Mar 2024`
- No filler phrases: "responsible for", "helped with", "worked on", "involved in"

## Keyword Density

- Top 10 JD keywords should appear 2–4 times across the resume in meaningful context
- Skills section: comma-separated tokens

## Score Weights

| Category                          | Weight |
| --------------------------------- | ------ |
| Required keyword coverage         | 40%    |
| Preferred keyword coverage        | 20%    |
| Section completeness              | 15%    |
| Bullet quality (action verb rate) | 10%    |
| Quantification rate               | 10%    |
| Title/role alignment              | 5%     |

Score ≥ 80% = Strong. 60–79% = Moderate. < 60% = Needs significant work.

## Bullet Pattern

`[Action Verb] + [Task/Technology] + [Result/Impact]`

- Weak: "Worked on backend APIs"
- Strong: "Engineered RESTful APIs in Node.js, reducing average response latency by 34%"
